# Code for America / Code for Dallas Email Draft

**Subject:** Pegboard — open-source civic transparency platform, looking for collaborators

**Body:**

Hi,

I'm reaching out to introduce Pegboard, an open-source civic transparency platform we've been building, and to see if there's interest in collaborating.

**What it is:** Pegboard ingests public government data — votes, bills, campaign finance, budgets, lobbying disclosures — and loads it into a Neo4j knowledge graph. The goal is to make civic data navigable: type in a representative's name and see their votes, donors, sponsored bills, and committee memberships in one place, all sourced from public records.

**What's built:** We have a working Python ingestor framework with scrapers for Congress members, congressional bills, and FEC campaign contributions. The graph schema models relationships between officials, legislation, donors, committees, and lobbyists. We're building a Svelte frontend for visual navigation (representative profile cards, budget treemaps, vote timelines).

**Why we're reaching out:** The platform is designed so any city can be added by writing a single Python ingestor file. We have issues specced out for Austin, Chicago, NYC, LA, and Houston city council ingestors — each one a self-contained contribution. We're looking for people who know their city's open data landscape: which APIs work, where the data quirks are, what data is available but not accessible.

If anyone in your brigade has experience with local government open data or is looking for a civic tech project to contribute to, we'd love to connect. The project is AGPL-3.0 licensed and all contributions are welcome.

Happy to do a short demo or walkthrough for anyone interested.

GitHub: [link]

Best,
[Name]
